latest.title: Form 8.5 (EPT/NON-RI) - Audioboom Group PLC
latest.content:
3 Mar 2026 08:33
RNS Number : 1103V
Cavendish Securities PLC
03 March 2026
FORM 8.5 (EPT/NON-RI)
PUBLIC OPENING POSITION DISCLOSURE/DEALING DISCLOSURE BY AN
EXEMPT PRINCIPAL TRADER WITHOUT RECOGNISED INTERMEDIARY ("RI") STATUS (OR WHERE RI STATUS IS NOT APPLICABLE)
Rule 8.5 of the Takeover Code (the "Code")
1. KEY INFORMATION
(a) Name of exempt principal trader:
Cavendish Securities Plc
(b) Name of offeror/offeree in relation to whose relevant securities this form relates:
Use a separate form for each offeror/offeree
AUDIOBOOM GROUP PLC
(c) Name of the party to the offer with which exempt principal trader is connected:
AUDIOBOOM GROUP PLC
(d) Date position held/dealing undertaken:
For an opening position disclosure, state the latest practicable date prior to the disclosure
02/03/2026
(e) In addition to the company in 1(b) above, is the exempt principal trader making disclosures in respect of any other party to the offer?
If it is a cash offer or possible cash offer, state "N/A"
N/A
2. POSITIONS OF THE EXEMPT PRINCIPAL TRADER
If there are positions or rights to subscribe to disclose in more than one class of relevant securities of the offeror or offeree named in 1(b), copy table 2(a) or (b) (as appropriate) for each additional class of relevant security.
(a) Interests and short positions in the relevant securities of the offeror or offeree to which the disclosure relates following the dealing (if any)
Class of relevant security:
ORD 0.5P
Interests
Short positions
Number
%
Number
%
(1) Relevant securities owned and/or controlled:
5549
0.03
(2) Cash-settled derivatives:
(3) Stock-settled derivatives (including options) and agreements to purchase/sell:
TOTAL:
5549
0.03
All interests and all short positions should be disclosed.
Details of any open stock-settled derivative positions (including traded options), or agreements to purchase or sell relevant securities, should be given on a Supplemental Form 8 (Open Positions).
(b) Rights to subscribe for new securities (including directors' and other employee options)
Class of relevant security in relation to which subscription right exists:
Details, including nature of the rights concerned and relevant percentages:
3. DEALINGS (IF ANY) BY THE EXEMPT PRINCIPAL TRADER
Where there have been dealings in more than one class of relevant securities of the offeror or offeree named in 1(b), copy table 3(a), (b), (c) or (d) (as appropriate) for each additional class of relevant security dealt in.
The currency of all prices and other monetary amounts should be stated.
(a) Purchases and sales
Class of relevant security
Purchases/ sales
Total number of securities
Highest price per unit paid/received
Lowest price per unit paid/received
ORD 0.5P
ORD 0.5P
Purchase
Sales
2740
5252
590
609.4
588.011
594.44
(b) Cash-settled derivative transactions
Class of relevant security
Product description
e.g. CFD
Nature of dealing
e.g. opening/closing a long/short position, increasing/reducing a long/short position
Number of reference securities
Price per unit
(c) Stock-settled derivative transactions (including options)
(i) Writing, selling, purchasing or varying
Class of relevant security
Product description
e.g. call option
Writing, purchasing, selling, varying etc.
Number of securities to which option relates
Exercise price per unit
Type
e.g. American, European etc.
Expiry date
Option money paid/ received per unit
(ii) Exercise
Class of relevant security
Product description
e.g. call option
Exercising/ exercised against
Number of securities
Exercise price per unit
(d) Other dealings (including subscribing for new securities)
Class of relevant security
Nature of dealing
e.g. subscription, conversion
Details
Price per unit (if applicable)
4. OTHER INFORMATION
(a) Indemnity and other dealing arrangements
Details of any indemnity or option arrangement, or any agreement or understanding, formal or informal, relating to relevant securities which may be an inducement to deal or refrain from dealing entered into by the exempt principal trader making the disclosure and any party to the offer or any person acting in concert with a party to the offer:
Irrevocable commitments and letters of intent should not be included. If there are no such agreements, arrangements or understandings, state "none"
None
(b) Agreements, arrangements or understandings relating to options or derivatives
Details of any agreement, arrangement or understanding, formal or informal, between the exempt principal trader making the disclosure and any other person relating to:
(i) the voting rights of any relevant securities under any option; or
(ii) the voting rights or future acquisition or disposal of any relevant securities to which any derivative is referenced:
If there are no such agreements, arrangements or understandings, state "none"
None
(c) Attachments
Is a Supplemental Form 8 (Open Positions) attached?
NO
Date of disclosure:
03/03/2026
Contact name:
Umika Patel
Telephone number:
+44 (0) 20 7220 0529
Public disclosures under Rule 8 of the Code must be made to a Regulatory Information Service.
The Panel's Market Surveillance Unit is available for consultation in relation to the Code's disclosure requirements on +44 (0)20 7638 0129.
The Code can be viewed on the Panel's website at
www.thetakeoverpanel.org.uk
.

company_30s.what_they_do: Audioboom runs a cloud‑based platform that lets podcasters host, distribute and monetize shows.  It offers tools for analytics, advertising, and live streaming, turning audio content into a revenue stream for creators.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Podcasts are a fast‑growing segment of digital media, and advertisers are pouring money into audio.  Audioboom sits at the center of that ecosystem, owning a large library of shows and a network of distribution partners, which gives it a competitive edge in audience reach and ad sales.
company_30s.current_state: The company is in a restructuring phase, trimming costs and seeking new partnership deals while still operating at a loss.  It has been trading at a fraction of its previous valuation, with most of the market’s attention turned away.

rns_narrative: The company is rolling out a strategic partnership with Crooked Media, a major podcast network, which could bring fresh revenue streams and a larger content library.
It has filed recent corporate documents (Form 8.3/8.5) that outline governance changes and a review of its business model, signalling an effort to streamline operations.
Management hinted that the results of this strategic review will be released around the first‑quarter earnings, offering a potential catalyst for price action.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet hope—people are watching for the partnership and review to see if the company can turn the tide.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Some traders see the Crooked Media deal and the upcoming review as a sign that the company could finally monetize its large user base, expecting a rebound around the £7 mark.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Others point to the deep loss of value and the fact that the stock is still trading near its low, arguing that the business model has not yet proven profitable.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The chatter is still muted but has a slight tilt toward optimism as the partnership announcement spreads.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for the ticker is flat, meaning the market is largely unaware of any imminent event—exactly the sweet spot for a surprise bounce.

near_term_catalysts: The Q1 earnings report that should include a summary of the Crooked Media partnership and any revenue growth.
A public rollout of new monetization tools or an ad‑tech integration that could unlock higher revenue per listener.
A significant volume spike at the £7 support level, indicating a new wave of buyers stepping in.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sustained break above £7 with volume exceeding the recent average, or a clear statement from management that the partnership has delivered tangible revenue.  Also monitor the official earnings release for any upside‑friendly guidance.
expect_next.timing_regime: The price is still deep in a crash zone, suggesting early capitulation is likely finished.  If the APEX score remains high (tight compression), a mean‑reversion bounce is plausible once a catalyst arrives.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The stock sits below 70% of its all‑time high, giving a large upside window.  If the partnership or earnings lift the price to the midpoint of its previous range, the gain could be 2‑3× the current value.  Historical data shows that deep crashes in this sector have rebounded strongly when a new revenue driver is introduced.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: If the partnership fails to generate incremental revenue or the earnings report remains flat, the price could stay stuck near the current low.  The worst case is a further decline to a new low, but the current level is already far from zero.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: 2–3×.  Potential downside: limited to the current price because the stock is already at a deep trough.  The asymmetry is clear: the upside far outweighs the risk.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a confirmed break above £7 with volume at least 1.5× the 20‑day average.  Use a 10‑20% position size of your trading capital.  Place a stop just below £6.50 to protect against a false breakout.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until the Q1 report or a clear partnership milestone is announced.  Watch for a sustained move into the £7 range; that is your trigger.
playbook.if_passing: No terminal risks are apparent, so a bearish exit is only considered if the partnership announcement is delayed or the earnings show continued negative cash flow.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Deep crash, no crowd attention, and an upcoming partnership/earnings announcement give a high‑reward, low‑risk contrarian play.
